channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man detained in Islamabad over viral social media post about Tarnol railway crossing
item.description: Islamabad police have taken a citizen into custody after he allegedly shared a viral social media post comparing the Tarnol Railway Crossing to the Strait of Hormuz. According to the First Information...

item.title: How to Avoid Costly Mistakes When Partnering for African Expansion
item.description: As foreign direct investment into Africa continues to grow, reaching $97 billion in 2024, many investors still face significant risks due to poor due diligence. Trust in local systems can be weak, wit...

item.title: Norway proposes law to restrict social media use for children under 16
item.description: Norway is set to introduce legislation this year that would prevent children under the age of 16 from using social media. The government aims to ensure a proper childhood for young people, prioritisin...

item.title: 65 Election Duty Officers in West Bengal Approach Supreme Court After Deletion from Voter Rolls
item.description: In a notable development ahead of the West Bengal Assembly Election 2026, sixty-five poll officials on election duty have approached the Supreme Court after their names were deleted from the Special I...

item.title: UAE Reassures Kenya of Uninterrupted Trade Amid Red Sea Delays
item.description: The UAE has reassured Kenya that its trade and travel links remain unaffected despite security concerns in the region, particularly in the Strait of Hormuz. UAE Minister of State, Saeed bin Mubarak Al...

item.title: Study finds widespread last-minute NHS surgery cancellations, many potentially avoidable
item.description: Research into elective surgery across 91 NHS trusts in England has revealed that around one in 10 operations are cancelled with less than 24 hours’ notice, with a further 9% postponed at the pre-opera...

item.title: Iranian Gunboat Fires at Container Ship in Strait of Hormuz, Crew Safe
item.description: An Iranian gunboat fired upon a container ship off the coast of Oman on Wednesday, causing significant damage to the bridge, but no fires or environmental impact were reported, and all crew members re...

item.title: IMF Suspends Kenya Loan Talks Pending Response to Corruption Governance Report
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has paused discussions on a new loan programme for Kenya, citing unresolved concerns around corruption and governance. According to the IMF’s African Department, ...

item.title: South African Police Commissioner Fannie Masemola Suspended Pending Court Case Over R360 Million Tender
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has placed National Police Commissioner Fannie Masemola on precautionary suspension with immediate effect following serious charges linked to a R360 million health services t...

item.title: Key NBA players' injuries shape crucial Game 3 matchups
item.description: Injuries are playing a pivotal role in the NBA playoffs as teams prepare for Game 3 on Friday. San Antonio Spurs await the return of Victor Wembanyama, who is recovering from a concussion sustained in...

item.title: US and Iran delegations likely to arrive in Islamabad for second round of mediated peace talks
item.description: In an important diplomatic development, delegations from the United States and Iran are expected to travel to Islamabad for a second round of peace talks, according to official sources. The Iranian de...

item.title: LRT-2 Santolan Station Provides Free Parking to Encourage Public Transit Use
item.description: Good news for commuters! Starting April 27, passengers of the Light Rail Transit System 2 (LRT-2) can park for free at the Santolan Station. This initiative, spearheaded by the Department of Transport...

item.title: NDIS Eligibility Rules: New Criteria for Accessing Support from 2028
item.description: The Australian government has announced a major overhaul of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), with new eligibility rules expected to impact more than one in five current participants. T...

item.title: Inquest into Baby's Death Highlights Failures in NHS Care During High-Risk Home Birth
item.description: An inquest into the tragic death of a seven-day-old baby, Poppy Hope Lomas, has revealed significant failings in the care provided by NHS midwives during a high-risk home birth. The baby died on 26 Oc...

item.title: Accord Party Clarifies Leadership Status, Confirms Imumolen Expelled
item.description: The Accord Party don clear road on the matter wey dem talk say dey cause wahala inside the party. According to the party, their 2023 presidential candidate, Christopher Imumolen, no dey inside the par...

item.title: Living the Anzac Spirit Beyond Commemoration
item.description: Peter Tinley, president of RSL Australia and former SAS squadron commander, reflects on the enduring significance of the Anzac spirit. He emphasises that Anzac values — courage, commitment, teamwork, ...

item.title: Constitutional Court to Review Public Participation in NHI Act
item.description: From 5-7 May 2026, South Africa's Constitutional Court will hear two pivotal challenges to the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act, brought by the Board of Healthcare Funders and the Western Cape gove...

item.title: Atiku's Move to ADC: Will It Affect His Political Future?
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar's recent hint at another presidential bid in 2027, this time under the African Democratic Congress (ADC), has stirred political discussions in Nigeria. Atiku, who ...

item.title: Ruto Commends Museveni's Strategic Approach to Uganda's Oil Resources
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto has expressed his admiration for Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni's strategic management of the country's oil resources. Speaking at the Africa We Build Summit in Nairob...

item.title: Recent Bestselling Books in Canada: A Weekly Roundup
item.description: This report presents a snapshot of the bestselling books in Canada for the week of April 18, 2024, with comparisons to previous weeks. The list encompasses various categories including fiction, non-fi...
